.ddx/attachments/ddx-b790449b/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-b790449b — no changes this attempt.

## Why this bead was not advanced in one execute-bead pass

The bead asks for `ddx work` to auto-route execution without any project
config. Read literally, the eight acceptance criteria require:

  AC1  Zero-config drain succeeds (project-side change + upstream relax)
  AC2  Reword the under-specified error to point at the global config
  AC3  Enable the triage complexity gate by default + opt-out flag
  AC4  Auto-tier selection from bead metadata (labels/kind/scope)
  AC5  Coarse-bead handling: decompose, OR emit a NEW `needs_triage`
       execution status (distinct from `execution_failed`)
  AC6  Failure recovery: retry-in-tier vs escalate-to-next-tier
  AC7  New integration test with a temp project + minimal global config
  AC8  Doc update presenting zero-config flow as the default

Each of AC3, AC4, and AC5 is a feature-sized piece of work on its own:

  - AC4 (auto-tier selection) implements a tier-inference engine over
    bead metadata. There is no such engine today; routing currently
    accepts operator intent or rejects with `routing under-specified`.
    This overlaps directly with the open endpoint-first routing redesign
    (memory: project_endpoint_routing_design — 2026-04-21: kill
    named-provider profiles, move to endpoints + live discovery + fuzzy
    model match). Implementing tier inference now without aligning with
    that redesign would either be redone or block it.

  - AC5 (`needs_triage` status) introduces a new ExecuteBeadStatus value
    that cuts across:
      internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go     (status enum + classify)
      internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go       (loop handling + retry)
      internal/bead/store.go                    (close vs unclaim policy)
      cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop.go             (operator output)
      docs/                                     (status table)
    plus the matching test surface. This cannot share a commit with the
    routing fix without conflating two reviewable changes.

  - AC3 (triage gate default-on) is a small toggle, but the gate
    function `w.ComplexityGate` is currently injected from outside and
    is `nil` in the CLI path (internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:506).
    Enabling it by default requires either a default ComplexityGate
    implementation (which is itself a dispatcher into AC4 inference) or
    a stub that always returns `shouldClaim=true` — the latter is
    indistinguishable from disabled and would not satisfy the AC.

The `routing under-specified` error itself is enforced in the upstream
fizeau library at:

  $GOMODCACHE/.../fizeau@v0.9.28/service_execute.go:192

DDx is currently on github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.9.28. The
short-circuit fires when *all* of Harness, Profile, ModelRef, Model,
Provider are empty. AC1 cannot be met purely on the DDx side without
either (a) populating one of those fields from a sensible default
(which is AC4) or (b) a fizeau release that lets the engine's
ResolveRoute see a "global default_provider" hint. Per memory
(feedback_no_local_replace), DDx must always import a properly tagged
agent release, so a `replace` directive is not available even
temporarily.

## What was verified before stopping

  - The under-specified error string is upstream-only — it does not
    occur in any DDx Go file (Grep across cli/, internal/, cmd/).
  - The flag set on `ddx agent work` already exposes --profile,
    --provider, --harness, --model, --model-ref, --effort. The user's
    report shows `--profile cheap` still failing, which is a separate
    bug from the bead's main thrust: profile is set as a non-empty
    flag default (agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, agent_cmd.go:1563) but
    is being passed through `singleTierAttempt` which uses
    CLIOverrides without setting Profile (agent_cmd.go:1716–1725).
    That single-line gap is plausibly the proximate cause of the
    `--profile cheap` regression but solving it does not satisfy the
    broader bead.
  - Triage gate disabled-by-default is at execute_bead_loop.go:506;
    the gate is a function field on ExecuteBeadWorker. There is no
    in-tree default implementation.

## Suggested decomposition

Recommend splitting this bead into the following children before any
implementation pass attempts AC1:

  ddx-XXXX  Profile flag passthrough fix: ensure --profile/--provider/
            --model-ref reach singleTierAttempt's CLIOverrides
            (single small commit + regression test). Likely closes the
            `--profile cheap` workaround failure.

  ddx-XXXX  Improve under-specified error: when global agent config has
            providers but the request has no routing inputs, point the
            operator at the global default_provider rather than at the
            per-project flag list. Coordinate with fizeau release.

  ddx-XXXX  Tier inference engine over bead metadata
            (kind/labels/file-touch estimate). Aligns with the
            endpoint-first routing redesign.

  ddx-XXXX  ExecuteBeadStatusNeedsTriage status + classify rules +
            close/unclaim policy + tests.

  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier
            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.

  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).

  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

If a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to
just AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default
harness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,
treating the absence of project config as "use global default_provider"
rather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.

Per the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently
execute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a
dispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.

            close/unclaim policy + tests.

  ddx-XXXX  Default-on ComplexityGate implementation (depends on tier
            inference) + opt-out flag --no-triage-gate.

  ddx-XXXX  Zero-config integration test (depends on the above).

  ddx-XXXX  Doc update: getting-started zero-config flow.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

If a single follow-up attempt is desired, it should narrow the bead to
just AC1 + AC2 with an explicit hand-off: the agent picks a default
harness/provider from the global agent config when nothing else is set,
treating the absence of project config as "use global default_provider"
rather than as a hard error. AC3–AC8 should be filed as sibling beads.

Per the bead's own AC #5, a coarse bead that no tier can confidently
execute is supposed to surface for operator triage rather than burn a
dispatch slot. This rationale is exactly that signal.

=====================================

The migration tool and tests are already implemented and merged into main
prior to this attempt. The previous attempt's commit a6cb9534
("feat(bead): add --dry-run to migrate and large-fixture split test
[ddx-cb2eb7e3]") landed on main and is an ancestor of this worktree's
HEAD (df2cea94).

The earlier review BLOCK ("diff contains only execution evidence files")
appears to have inspected the wrong diff. The actual implementation is
present and exercised by passing tests; no further code changes are
required to satisfy the acceptance criteria.

AC verification (this attempt)
------------------------------

1. Command + --dry-run flag:
   - cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go defines `ddx bead migrate` with `--dry-run`
     and `--json` flags.

2. Repo split (beads.jsonl < 4MB, archive populated, attachments
   populated):
   - Live repo state: .ddx/beads.jsonl is 153K, .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl
     is 2.7M, .ddx/attachments/ has many per-bead event sidecars
     (ddx-0a33bc5f, ddx-0a651925, ddx-0b9c980a, ...).

3. Re-run is a no-op:
   - `ddx bead migrate --json` on the live repo after the prior
     migration returns
       {"EventsExternalized": 0, "Archived": 0}
     and exit 0.

4. `ddx bead show` works for migrated (active + archived) beads:
   - cli/cmd/bead_migrate_test.go::TestMigrateCommand asserts that
     `bead show ddx-c1` (archived) returns the bead JSON.

5. `ddx bead list` / ready / blocked still work post-migration:
   - Same test asserts archived beads (ddx-c1, ddx-c2) and the open
     bead (ddx-open) all appear in the default list output.

6. Test runs migration on a synthetic fixture and verifies the split:
   - cli/cmd/bead_migrate_test.go and cli/internal/bead/migrate_test.go
     exercise the migration end-to-end against a synthetic JSONL fixture
     and verify event externalization, archival, and idempotency.

Test run
--------
   cd cli && go test ./cmd -run 'TestMigrateCommand' -count=1
   ok  	github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd	0.059s

No source changes are made in this attempt because none are needed.

output:
ID:       ddx-e012af90
Title:    feat(escalation): add PostLadderExhaustionHook type, wiring, routing, and recovery:manual bypass
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Parent:   ddx-63155d5c
Desc:     PROBLEM
  execute_bead_loop.go increments consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey when per-bead
  budget exhausts (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1459), but when the
  counter reaches >= 2 there is no PostLadderExhaustionHook to dispatch
  auto-recovery. The recovery:manual label opt-out does not exist. Failure-class
  routing (spec_gap → REFRAME; too_large → DECOMPOSE; persistent execution_failed
  → REFRAME-first) is also absent.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1459 calls
  incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions but the comment marks the subsequent
  hook as "sister bead ddx-63155d5c" which was never implemented.
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2847 (applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation)
  and lines 2796-2845 (applyNoChangesSmartRetry) both fall to status=proposed
  when ladder is exhausted, with no auto-recovery attempted.

PROPOSED FIX
  1. Add PostLadderExhaustionHook type + RecoveryFailureClass and RecoveryPath
     enums in a new file cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook.go.
  2. Add PostLadderExhaustionHook field to ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime.
  3. Wire hook invocation in execute_bead_loop.go immediately after the
     consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey counter reaches >= 2:
     - Read label slice; if "recovery:manual" present, skip hook and park proposed.
     - Determine failure class from the last attempt's stop condition.
     - Call hook (nil hook or not-implemented stub → fall through to proposed).
  4. Stub hook implementations return PostLadderExhaustionResult{attempted:false}.
  5. Two unit tests in cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook_test.go.

NON-SCOPE
  Actual reframer/decomposer agent dispatch (child beads 2 and 3).
  Circuit-breaker cost cap (child bead 4).
  Prompt templates (child beads 2 and 3).
Accept:   1. cli/internal/agent/recovery_hook.go exists and defines: PostLadderExhaustionHook function type with signature func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, failureClass RecoveryFailureClass) (*PostLadderExhaustionResult, error); RecoveryFailureClass enum (SpecGap, TooLarge, PersistentExecutionFailed); RecoveryPath enum (Reframe, Decompose); PostLadderExhaustionResult struct with fields: attempted bool, succeeded bool, path RecoveryPath, costUSD float64.
2. ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime in execute_bead_loop.go has a PostLadderExhaustionHook field of the new type.
3. Hook fires in execute_bead_loop.go when consecutiveLadderExhaustionsValue(bead) >= 2 (immediately after the increment at the per-bead-budget-exhausted path, line ~1459).
4. If bead has label "recovery:manual", hook invocation is skipped and bead parks to status=proposed with a NeedsHumanMeta reason of "recovery:manual label set".
5. Failure class is derived from the last attempt outcome: review_spec_gap or review_missing_acceptance → SpecGap; review_too_large → TooLarge; all other persistent failures → PersistentExecutionFailed.
6. When PostLadderExhaustionHook is nil or returns (nil result, nil error) or attempted=false, execution falls through to the existing status=proposed path unchanged.
7. TestConsecutiveLadderExhaustionsCounter passes: verifies counter increments on per-bead-budget-exhausted and hook fires at threshold 2.
8. TestRecoveryManualLabel_SkipsAutoRecovery passes: verifies bead with label "recovery:manual" parks to proposed without invoking the hook.
9. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
10. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-24a9286f/events.jsonl:13:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead ddx-24a9286f — sidecar .ddx.yaml schema design — is already complete.

What is done
------------
The design document exists at:
  docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-025-sidecar-ddx-yaml-schema.md

It was authored on a previous attempt and merged via commit cda66189
("docs: sidecar .ddx.yaml schema design (TD-025) [ddx-24a9286f]"), which is
an ancestor of the current base revision f21bd2b6 (verified with
`git merge-base --is-ancestor cda66189 HEAD`). The file is tracked
(`git ls-files` confirms) and present in the worktree.

TD-025 covers all three items called out in the bead description:

1. Pairing rule
   Section "Pairing Rule" specifies append-not-replace
   (foo.png ↔ foo.png.ddx.yaml, diagram.excalidraw ↔
   diagram.excalidraw.ddx.yaml). Same-directory requirement,
   case-sensitivity, glob discovery (`**/*.ddx.yaml`), and rationale
   for append-vs-replace are all documented.

2. Conflict rule (frontmatter + sidecar)
   Section "Conflict Rule" sets the strict policy: markdown uses
   embedded frontmatter exclusively, non-markdown uses sidecars
   exclusively. A `*.md.ddx.yaml` sidecar alongside a `*.md` file is a
   validation error; sidecar is ignored to avoid duplicate IDs. Full
   conflict table with outcomes is provided.

3. Orphan-check participation in `ddx doc validate`
   Section "Orphan-Check Participation" specifies parity with markdown
   artifacts, the role of `generated_by` edges in suppressing orphan
   warnings (provenance-only but does count for orphan suppression),
   and the distinct "dangling sidecar" hard-error rule for sidecars
   without paired assets. Validation summary table at the end
   enumerates every check and severity.

What is blocking
----------------
Nothing is blocking; there is no further work for this bead. The
prior review note ("REVIEW:BLOCK — diff contains only execution
manifest/result metadata; the actual sidecar schema design document
referenced as cda66189 in result_rev is not part of the changed
files") appears to have inspected the execution-metadata diff rather
than the merged source tree. The artifact named in result_rev is
present in the repository at HEAD.

What a follow-up attempt would need
-----------------------------------
A follow-up attempt should not regenerate or rewrite TD-025 — that
would churn already-approved design content and risk diverging from
the consumers of the schema (FEAT-005 / FEAT-007 scanner refactor in
later beads). If the reviewer believes specific gaps remain, those
gaps should be enumerated as concrete acceptance items
(e.g., "specify behavior when sidecar YAML is malformed", "define
sidecar schema-version field") so the next attempt has a precise
delta to address. Re-queuing on the same description will produce
the same conclusion.

spoke lifecycle, fan-out client, federated GraphQL schema, SvelteKit UI, backend chaos
suite, and Playwright e2e — well beyond a bounded-context attempt (>>500 LOC across many
unrelated packages).

Per execute-bead Step 0, this attempt decomposes rather than implements. Children created
and linked under ddx-a038a090 with `ddx bead dep add` to capture the dependency DAG from
the plan:

  B14.2  ddx-1bbd1096  cli/internal/federation pkg: state types + persistence + schema_version
                       (deps: B14.1)
  B14.3  ddx-ab7f6184  Hub HTTP API + --hub-mode + ts-net policy + version handshake
                       (deps: B14.2)
  B14.4  ddx-238880c4  Spoke lifecycle: --hub=, idempotent register, jittered heartbeat,
                       URL-change, deregister
                       (deps: B14.3)
  B14.6a ddx-89bc2db4  Fan-out client/merger: timeout, max-concurrency, partial-result,
                       per-node errors
                       (deps: B14.3)
  B14.6b ddx-586d9431  Federated GraphQL: federationNodes, federatedBeads/Runs/Projects
                       + routing-metadata fields
                       (deps: B14.6a)
  B14.7  ddx-e869a89c  Frontend: /federation route + node-picker + ?scope=federation

                       (deps: B14.6b)
  B14.8a ddx-13e19750  Backend chaos/integration suite (hub crash, stale spoke, dup reg,
                       newer schema, slow timeout, hub-restart-rebuild)
                       (deps: B14.6b)
  B14.8b ddx-e59bff83  Playwright e2e for 2-node federation + ts-net guard AC
                       (deps: B14.7, B14.8a)

chaos and Playwright e2e have different infra needs and tier suggestions (strong vs
cheap-do + strong-review).

attempt; no commit produced. Subsequent execute-bead attempts will pick up B14.1 first
(it is the only child with no deps).

rationale: verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/bead ./internal/checks && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestSubmitWithPreMergeChecks'

No new code changes were made in this attempt because the implementation for bead ddx-3e60fd84 is already present in this worktree history at commit 43efd67eb74320cfb45e93ae96b526d1d97812f3 (`feat(agent): pre-merge checks gate with checks_bypass annotation [ddx-3e60fd84]`), which is an ancestor of the current checkpoint.

Evidence inspected:
- cli/internal/agent/checks_premerge.go implements RunPreMergeChecks, AppendPreMergeChecksEvents, and PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go defines SubmitWithPreMergeChecks, which runs pre_merge checks before submit, preserves refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/ on block/error, and records checks-blocked/checks-bypass events.
- cli/cmd/try.go and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go route ddx try / work landing through SubmitWithPreMergeChecks.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go routes server-managed work landing through evaluateGatesAndSubmit; this committed slice includes the single-bead and CLI loop integration paths, while server gate integration remains governed by its existing required-gate helper.
- cli/internal/bead/checks_bypass.go parses checks_bypass annotations and rejects empty reasons.
- cli/internal/agent/checks_premerge_e2e_test.go covers dummy-fail abort/preserve/evidence/event, dummy-fail bypass merge/event, and missing-reason rejection.
- cli/internal/bead/checks_bypass_test.go covers parser success and validation failures.

Verification performed in this attempt:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead ./internal/checks && go test ./internal/agent -run 'TestSubmitWithPreMergeChecks'` passed.
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/bead ./internal/checks ./cmd` failed only in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd: TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier still reports zero review events, and installation acceptance tests expect cli/build/ddx to exist.
- `cd cli && go test ./...` reproduced the same cmd failures; all touched internal packages passed.
- `lefthook run pre-commit` passed with no matching staged files.

The full-suite red state is outside this bead's touched implementation and matches the pre-existing failure noted in the landed bead commit.

the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\n+just to refresh timestamps.\n+\n+## How to regenerate\n+\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\n+format:\n+\n+```bash\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+  --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\n+  --status   success \\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+```\n+\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\n+bytes alone.\n+\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\n+\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). b/scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh\n@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@\n+#!/usr/bin/env bash\n+# capture-omlx-fixture.sh — regenerate a testdata/omlx-wire fixture from a\n+# live omlx endpoint. Wraps `ddx agent run` with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1\n+# (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14 — bead agent-f237e07b) and reshapes the wire\n+# dump into the fixture JSONL format the consumer-side smoke test expects.\n+#\n+# Usage:\n+#   scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\n+#     --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\n+#     --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\n+#     --prompt   \"Reply with the word ready.\" \\\n+#     --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\n+#\n+# Required tools: ddx (v0.6.0-alpha22+), jq.\n+#\n+# What the script does:\n+#   1. Runs an isolated ddx agent invocation against the given omlx endpoint\n+#      with AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 so the whole\n+#      SSE stream is captured to a sidecar wire file.\n+#   2. Extracts every `dir==\"response\"` body chunk in arrival order and emits\n+#      one {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...} record per chunk.\n+#   3. Prepends a {\"kind\":\"meta\",...} record with the endpoint, model, prompt,\n+#      and a user-supplied expected outcome so the replay test knows what to\n+#      assert.\n+#\n+# Fixtures are checked in. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test. Authors may\n+# need to hand-edit content_contains / error_contains after inspecting the\n+# captured body.\n+jq -cn \\\n+  --arg name \"$name\" \\\n+  --arg desc \"Captured $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ) from $endpoint model=$model prompt=$prompt\" \\\n+  --arg status \"$status\" \\\n+  '{kind:\"meta\", name:$name, description:$desc,\n+    expected:{status:$status, error_must_not_contain:\"unexpected end of JSON input\"}}' \\\n+  >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+# Frame records — every response chunk in capture order. AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE\n+# with STREAM_FULL=1 emits one {\"dir\":\"response\",\"body\":\"…\"} record per read.\n+jq -c 'select(.dir==\"response\" and (.body|type==\"string\") and (.body|length>0))\n+       | {kind:\"frame\", body:.body}' \\\n+  \"$wire_file\" >> \"$tmp_out\"\n+\n+mv \"$tmp_out\" \"$out\"\n+echo \"wrote $(wc -l < \"$out\") records to $out\" >&2","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"51e05227-4794-4fdf-b297-f2720148e2a0","uuid":"ad85bb2f-b87a-4555-85d6-b2e11af2638d","timestamp":"2026-04-18T04:31:26.286Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"diff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..3438eff3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,230 @@\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"bufio\"\n+\t\"encoding/json\"\n+\t\"io\"\n+\t\"net/http\"\n+\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"path/filepath\"\n+\t\"strings\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\t\"time\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+// omlxFixture is the parsed representation of a testdata/omlx-wire/*.jsonl file.\n+// Each fixture captures a recorded vidar-omlx SSE response plus the outcome the\n+// consumer must produce when it replays. Fixtures are authored either by hand\n+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by\n+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.\n+type omlxFixture struct {\n+\tMeta   omlxFixtureMeta\n+\tFrames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {\n+\tName        string              `json:\"name\"`\n+\tDescription string              `json:\"description\"`\n+\tExpected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:\"expected\"`\n+}\n+\n+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {\n+\t// Status is \"success\" or \"error\".\n+\tStatus string `json:\"status\"`\n+\t// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must\n+\t// not contain. This is the regression guard: \"unexpected end of JSON input\"\n+\t// is the exact signature of the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame defect (bead\n+\t// agent-f237e07b) that v0.3.14's sseCommentFilter fixed.\n+\tErrorMustNotContain string `json:\"error_must_not_contain\"`\n+\t// ContentContains is a substring the assembled content must contain on the\n+\t// happy path.\n+\tContentContains string `json:\"content_contains\"`\n+\t// FinishReason is the expected finish reason on the happy path.\n+\tFinishReason string `json:\"finish_reason\"`\n+\t// ErrorContains is a substring the surfaced error must contain on the\n+\t// error path — asserts the error is propagated rather than swallowed.\n+\tErrorContains string `json:\"error_contains\"`\n+}\n+\n+// loadOMLXFixture reads a JSONL fixture from testdata/omlx-wire/. The first\n+// line is the {\"kind\":\"meta\", ...} record; remaining {\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":...}\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"open fixture %s\", path)\n+\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\n+\n+\tvar fix omlxFixture\n+\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\n+\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\n+\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\n+\tfirst := true\n+\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n+\t\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\n+\t\tif line == \"\" {\n+\t\t\tcontinue\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tvar record struct {\n+\t\t\tKind string          `json:\"kind\"`\n+\t\t\tBody string          `json:\"body\"`\n+\t\t\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\"-\"`\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tswitch record.Kind {\n+\t\tcase \"meta\":\n+\t\t\tif !first {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\", path)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"parsing meta in %s: %v\", path, err)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\tcase \"frame\":\n+\t\t\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\n+\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\", path, record.Kind)\n+\t\t}\n+\t\tfirst = false\n+\t}\n+\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \"scanning fixture %s\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \"fixture %s: missing meta.name\", path)\n+\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \"fixture %s: no frame records\", path)\n+\treturn fix\n+}\n+\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\n+\tt.Helper()\n+\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n+\t\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\n+\t\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\n+\t\t// with text/event-stream.\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Cache-Control\", \"no-cache\")\n+\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Transfer-Encoding\", \"chunked\")\n+\t\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\n+\t\tfor _, body := range frames {\n+\t\t\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\n+\t\t\t\treturn\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tif flusher != nil {\n+\t\t\t\tflusher.Flush()\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\n+\t\t\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\n+\t\t\t// under a second total.\n+\t\t\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}))\n+\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\n+\treturn srv\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\n+//\n+// What this guards against:\n+//\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\n\\n`\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \"unexpected end of\n+//     JSON input\".\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\n+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by\n+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and\n+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and\n+//     specifically NOT \"unexpected end of JSON input\", which would mean the\n+//     SSE filter regressed).\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfixtureDir := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\")\n+\tcases := []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"}\n+\n+\tfor _, name := range cases {\n+\t\tname := name\n+\t\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\n+\t\t\tfix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))\n+\n+\t\t\tsrv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)\n+\n+\t\t\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\t\t\tBaseURL: srv.URL + \"/v1\",\n+\t\t\t\tAPIKey:  \"test\",\n+\t\t\t\tModel:   \"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\",\n+\t\t\t\t// Flavor \"omlx\" pins the capability table to the one used in\n+\t\t\t\t// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real\n+\t\t\t\t// dispatch path.\n+\t\t\t\tFlavor: \"omlx\",\n+\t\t\t})\n+\n+\t\t\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})\n+\t\t\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\t\t\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\t\t\t// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when\n+\t\t\t// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.\n+\t\t\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\t\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\t\t\tPrompt:  \"smoke test — omlx fixture replay\",\n+\t\t\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t\t\t})\n+\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\")\n+\n+\t\t\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\n+\t\t\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\n+\t\t\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\n+\t\t\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\")\n+\t\t\t}\n+\n+\t\t\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\n+\t\t\tcase \"success\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \"happy-path must produce no error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tcase \"error\":\n+\t\t\t\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\")\n+\t\t\t\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \"error-path must surface a non-empty error\")\n+\t\t\t\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \"\" {\n+\t\t\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\n+\t\t\t\t\t\t\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\")\n+\t\t\t\t}\n+\t\t\tdefault:\n+\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\n+\t\t\t}\n+\t\t})\n+\t}\n+}\n+\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\n+\tfor _, name := range []string{\"happy_path.jsonl\", \"error_path.jsonl\"} {\n+\t\tp := filepath.Join(\"testdata\", \"omlx-wire\", name)\n+\t\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\n+\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"missing fixture %s\", p)\n+\t\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \"%s must be a file\", p)\n+\t\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \"%s must not be empty\", p)\n+\t}\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\n+\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\n+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI\n+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is\n+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. It is gated\n+// by the `omlx_smoke` build tag so the default `go test ./...` never runs it.\n+//\n+// Run:\n+//\n+//\tOMLX_SMOKE_URL=http://vidar:1235/v1\\\n+//\tOMLX_SMOKE_MODEL=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\n+//\tgo test -tags omlx_smoke -run TestOMLXLiveSmoke -v ./cli/internal/agent\n+//\n+// This covers headers, etc.) that\n+// a recorded fixture cannot detect.\n+\n+package agent\n+\n+import (\n+\t\"os\"\n+\t\"testing\"\n+\n+\t\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent/provider/openai\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n+\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n+)\n+\n+func TestOMLXLiveSmoke(t *testing.T) {\n+\tbaseURL := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL\")\n+\tif baseURL == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Skip(\"OMLX_SMOKE_URL not set — live smoke requires an omlx endpoint\")\n+\t}\n+\tmodel := os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL\")\n+\tif model == \"\" {\n+\t\tt.Fatal(\"OMLX_SMOKE_MODEL is required when OMLX_SMOKE_URL is set\")\n+\t}\n+\n+\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\n+\t\tBaseURL: baseURL,\n+\t\tAPIKey:  os.Getenv(\"OMLX_SMOKE_API_KEY\"),\n+\t\tModel:   model,\n+\t\tFlavor:  \"omlx\",\n+\t})\n+\n+\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 120000})\n+\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\n+\tr.AgentProvider = provider\n+\n+\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\n+\t\tHarness: \"agent\",\n+\t\tPrompt:  \"Reply with the single word 'ready' and nothing else.\",\n+\t\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\n+\t})\n+\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n+\n+\t// The regression signature is the precise error v0.3.13 surfaced when\n+\t// omlx emitted keep-alive SSE comment frames during reasoning warmup.\n+\t// Any bump that reintroduces it must be caught here before it ships.\n+\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, \"unexpected end of JSON input\",\n+\t\t\"live omlx produced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature\")\n+\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \"live omlx smoke must close cleanly; error=%q\", result.Error)\n+\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Output, \"live omlx must return some content\")\n+}\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 00000000..1f3cbdfa\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\n+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures\n+\n+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\n+\n+## File format\n+\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\n+\n+- First line: `{\"kind\":\"meta\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\n+- Remaining lines: `{\"kind\":\"frame\",\"body\":\"<raw SSE body>\"}` — one frame per\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\n\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\n+  response in arrival order.\n+\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \"unexpected end of JSON input\" regression) and normal\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\n+\n+## When to regenerate\n+\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Regenerate only when the omlx server changes wire\n+# behavior (new frame type, header shift, chunk-size change) in a way the\n+# existing fixtures no longer represent.\n+\n+set -euo pipefail\n+\n+endpoint=\"\"\n+model=\"\"\n+prompt=\"\"\n+out=\"\"\n+name=\"\"\n+status=\"success\"\n+\n+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n+  case \"$1\" in\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --model)    model=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --out)      out=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --name)     name=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n+    --status)   status=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;  # success | error\n+    -h|--help)\n+      sed -n '2,28p' \"$0\"\n+      exit 0\n+      ;;\n+    *) echo \"unknown arg: $1\" >&2; exit 2 ;;\n+  esac\n+done\n+\n+for v in endpoint model prompt out; do\n+  if [[ -z \"${!v}\" ]]; then\n+    echo \"missing required --$v\" >&2\n+    exit 2\n+  fi\n+done\n+\n+command -v jq >/dev/null || { echo \"jq is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+command -v ddx >/dev/null || { echo \"ddx is required\" >&2; exit 2; }\n+\n+wire_file=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-wire.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\"' EXIT\n+\n+echo \"capturing wire dump from $endpoint → $wire_file\" >&2\n+\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 \\\n+AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_FILE=\"$wire_file\" \\\n+ddx agent run \\\n+  --harness agent \\\n+  --provider vidar-omlx \\\n+  --model \"$model\" \\\n+  --prompt-text \"$prompt\" \\\n+  --timeout 120s \\\n+  >/dev/null || {\n+    echo \"ddx agent run failed — fixture capture incomplete\" >&2\n+    echo \"wire dump (may still be useful): $wire_file\" >&2\n+    exit 1\n+  }\n+\n+[[ -z \"$name\" ]] && name=\"$(basename \"$out\" .jsonl)\"\n+\n+tmp_out=\"$(mktemp -t omlx-fixture.XXXXXX.jsonl)\"\n+trap 'rm -f \"$wire_file\" \"$tmp_out\"' EXIT\n+\n+# Meta record — expected outcome is a hint for the replay test.          -bench 'BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookup(LegacyScan)?Latency$' \
         -benchtime=80x -count=1
       BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookupLatency            p50=2.702 ms  p95=3.384 ms
       BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookupLegacyScanLatency  p50=13.19 ms  p95=15.12 ms
     Matches the recorded baseline within run-to-run noise.

3. "After the fix, p95 ≤ 50 ms in-process / ≤ 200 ms over HTTP"
   → TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget asserts in-process
     GetBeadSnapshot p95 ≤ 50 ms; measured this pass: p95 = 2.997 ms.
     TestGraphQLBeadLookupLatencyBudget asserts HTTP GraphQL bead(id:)
     p95 ≤ 200 ms; measured this pass: p95 = 3.267 ms.
     Both tests pass.

4. "Write-then-read: `ddx bead update` then GraphQL returns new value"
   → TestGraphQLBeadLookupReadsExternalCLIUpdateOnNextRequest
     (graphql_bead_lookup_perf_test.go). Test shells out to
     `go run . bead update <id> --title …` against the fixture's
     .ddx directory, then hits the GraphQL bead(id:) endpoint and asserts
     the new title is visible on the next request. Passes this pass.

5. "Concurrency-safe under interleaved bead(id:) and bead update"
   → TestGraphQLBeadLookupConcurrentQueriesAndUpdates spins up 8 workers ×
     20 iterations of interleaved GraphQL beadUpdate mutations and bead(id:)
     queries on the same fixture bead, asserting no panics, no torn reads,
     and consistent title/updatedAt fields. Passes this pass.

6. "Playwright e2e: click a bead row, assert sidepane shows title within 1 s"
   → cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/beads.spec.ts
     TC-014 ("clicking a bead row opens its detail panel") includes the
     1-second assertion added by 5a05c178:
       await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'First bead' }))
         .toBeVisible({ timeout: 1000 });

Implementation that drives the AC:

- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-99
    queryResolver.Bead now calls r.State.GetBeadSnapshot(id) instead of
    scanning every bead snapshot.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_node.go
    StateProvider interface gained GetBeadSnapshot(id).
- cli/internal/server/state.go
    ServerState gained beadIndexMu + beadIndex (beadID → {projectID, path}).
- cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go
    - GetBeadSnapshot uses the index + bead.Store.Get for O(1)/O(log n) lookup,
      falling back to a single per-project probe + index repair on index miss
      or stale-index mismatch.
    - rememberBeadLocation / forgetBeadLocation / lookupBeadLocation manage
      the index, warmed both by list traffic (GetBeadSnapshots) and by
      successful singleton lookups.
- cli/internal/server/graphql/integration_test.go
    testStateProvider gained GetBeadSnapshot to satisfy the interface.

Conclusion: nothing to commit. The bead contract is met in the base tree;
the review findings are outdated relative to c1822352. If the orchestrator
still re-queues this bead, the next attempt should re-run the above tests
against the current base-rev and compare line-by-line with the AC — the
artifacts will still be there.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-f7f09b6e/events.jsonl:3:{"actor":"erik","body":"Bead: ddx-f7f09b6e — Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)

Result: no changes needed; AC already satisfied by prior commits.

Audit against acceptance criteria
---------------------------------

AC1. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl is recognized as a valid bead collection.

  - cli/internal/bead/archive.go:11 declares
    BeadsArchiveCollection = "beads-archive".
  - cli/internal/bead/registry.go:126-130 registers the spec in the default
    registry with JSONLFile "beads-archive.jsonl" and LockDirName
    "beads-archive.lock".
  - The archive partner store opens via NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir,
    BeadsArchiveCollection) (archive.go:42), so the JSONL backend resolves
    it to .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl by the same registry path.

AC2. ddx bead show <id> finds beads in either active or archive.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:319 wires `bead show` to s.GetWithArchive.
  - Implementation: archive.go:164-177 — first tries the active Get, then
    falls back to the archivePartner. Active-collection guard at line 168
    prevents recursion when the caller is already on the archive collection.
  - Test: TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack
    (cli/internal/bead/archive_test.go:150-183) seeds an active and an
    archived bead, runs Archive(), and asserts GetWithArchive resolves both
    IDs and errors on a missing one. PASS.

AC3. ddx bead list has a --include-archive flag (or equivalent) and the
default behavior is documented.

  - cli/cmd/bead.go:846 declares the equivalent flag:
    cmd.Flags().Bool("all", false, "Include archived beads
    (.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl)").
  - Default behavior is to consult the archive partner via ListWithArchive
    (bead.go:808-812). The flag is a no-op kept for compatibility, with the
    behavior documented in the inline comment at bead.go:808-810.
  - Implementation: archive.go:182-209 — union with active-wins precedence,
    archive-missing tolerated as empty.
  - Test: TestArchiveListWithArchiveIncludesBoth (archive_test.go:216-245)
    exercises both the active-only List() and the merged ListWithArchive().

---

## Summary

All acceptance criteria for ddx-61f6c57d are already complete and verified.

### AC1: CI schema gate - COMPLETE ✓

- Schema defined: cli/internal/agent/schema/readiness-checks.schema.json
- Producer references schema: cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:639
- Consumer implements schema contract: readinessVerdict.UnmarshalJSON() (lines 97-125)
- CI validation: make readiness-schema in .github/workflows/ci.yml
- Tests pass:
  - TestReadinessChecksSchema (8 subtests)
  - TestReadinessVerdict_AcceptsBoolStringOrNull (6 subtests)
  - TestBuildPreClaimIntakePrompt_UsesDocumentedReadinessSchema

### AC2: Same-fingerprint escalation - COMPLETE ✓

- Default threshold: DefaultPreClaimWarnRepeatThreshold = 5
- Tracking: preClaimWarnRepeatState tracks fingerprints and counts
- Escalation: appendPreClaimIntakeWarning() emits loop.operator_attention event
- Worker stops looping: StopCondition set to OperatorAttention
- Test passes: TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold

### AC3 & AC4: Tests pass - COMPLETE ✓

- go test ./... passes
- lefthook run pre-commit passes

### Historical Context

This bead is part 2 of decomposed parent ddx-f9ddaa68. The three-part decomposition
addressed a production wedge where Fizeau's worker queue stalled (23 ready beads,
worker looping silently) due to schema drift in readiness_checks.verdict handling.

Prior work (ddx-12a6a072, ddx-066cceaf) implemented the core fixes:
- Fixed readinessVerdict bool/string/null decoder
- Aligned schema contract in producer prompt

This bead (part 2) was responsible for verifying that:
1. CI schema gate prevents future drift (via TestReadinessChecksSchema)
2. Escalation prevents stalls (via TestPreClaimWarnSameFingerprintEscalatesAfterThreshold)

Both mechanisms are fully implemented and tested.

Disposition: no commit this pass; recommend decomposing into 4–5 smaller beads.

(a) What is done in this pass
- Read the bead description and 12 acceptance criteria.
- Mapped the existing surface area:
  - GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql with gqlgen-generated resolvers; subscription pattern established in resolver_sub_worker.go / resolver_sub_bead.go / resolver_sub_exec.go (buffered channel + ctx-cancel goroutine).
  - Frontend GraphQL via graphql-request (queries) + graphql-ws (subscriptions) — see cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/gql/{client.ts,subscriptions.ts}. No Houdini.
  - Project home page at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (queue summary + 3 action cards). This is the host surface for the chat pane.
  - Sessions index at cli/internal/agent/session_index.go, sharded JSONL under .ddx/sessions/ (monthly). agentSessions GraphQL query already exists. This is the linkage target for AC5.
  - Agent execution entry at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunWithConfigViaService → executeOnService → records a session-index row). Streaming today is per-harness; the work streams events via worker progress, not per-prompt chunks.
  - Playwright e2e pattern in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/**/*.e2e.ts uses page.route('/graphql') interception with fixture data; no live backend.

(b) What is blocking a one-pass implementation
The bead bundles a feature that fans out across at least 5 independently-reviewable surfaces:

  1. Storage layer (.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}) — new Go package with tests, append-only invariants, project/operator scoping.
  2. GraphQL schema + resolvers — chatThreads/chatThread/chatMessages queries, chatThreadCreate/chatSend mutations, chatStream subscription. Requires schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regeneration of generated.go, models.go updates, four new resolver files, integration tests.
  3. service.Execute integration — chatSend must dispatch a session and stream chunks + tool calls back over the chatStream subscription. Today service.Execute is request/response with a non-streaming Result; per-chunk streaming over the subscription bus is new infrastructure. Either: (i) add a streaming variant of executeOnService, or (ii) tail the session's evidence stream and re-emit. Both have non-trivial design choices that belong in the design note before code lands.
  4. Frontend — chat pane component, thread list, harness/profile picker, streaming render, tool-call inline rendering, Stop button (AC7), cumulative cost header (AC5/AC8). Real-time updates via subscribeChatMessages helper. Roughly parallel in size to the workers detail page.
  5. Auth posture middleware (AC9) — even as a "single-operator localhost / tsnet for v1" stance, the design note must commit to a posture and a route-gate stub must exist so future auth is schema-local. The bead description flags this as load-bearing for the "remote ops" framing.
  6. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ answering the six subsidiary questions (persistence scope, harness selection, cost accountability, tool-execution scope, concurrency, auth). The bead's AC1 makes this a hard prerequisite — the implementation PR is supposed to *link* to a landed design note. The note must precede or accompany the implementation; it cannot be a postscript.
  7. Playwright e2e (AC10) — five-step scenario including two browser contexts on the same thread, cancel mid-flight, streamed chunk ordering. Mocking the GraphQL subscription side of this is non-trivial; the existing e2e suite mocks queries via page.route but does not exercise graphql-ws subscriptions.

A single execute-bead commit covering all of the above would either (i) be partial across most of those surfaces and red, or (ii) take many hours of focused work and produce a sprawling diff that the review gate cannot meaningfully verify in one pass. Both outcomes are worse than a clean decomposition.

Additional concrete unknowns the design note must resolve before code can land cleanly:
- Is the chatStream subscription chunk-level (assistant text deltas) or event-level (one event per tool call + one final assistant message)? This determines whether service.Execute needs a new streaming surface or can be wrapped post-hoc.
- For multi-operator on the same thread (AC6), is the second operator a read-only observer or an equal participant who can also send? The bead defaults to "two browsers on the same thread see each other within 2s" which implies multi-participant — but the cost-accountability and session-attribution model needs a per-message author identity that today's auth posture (single-operator) does not surface.
- Does chatSend run in the project worktree or a per-thread worktree? Bead suggests "same as ddx agent run" as the default, but if two threads chatSend concurrently they will race the worktree. This argues for per-thread worktree, which is a real cost (disk, fs setup) and belongs in the design note.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need

Recommend splitting this bead into the following sub-beads, executed in order:

  Sub-bead 1: Design note (docs/helix/02-design/chat-interface.md)
    - Picks Option A vs B vs C vs D and locks it.
    - Answers the six subsidiary questions.
    - Specifies the message/thread schema (manifest.json shape, messages.jsonl entry shape).
    - Specifies whether chatStream is chunk-level or event-level.
    - Specifies worktree scoping per thread.
    - Specifies auth posture for v1 and the gap to broader deployment.
    Size: 1 pass, design only. No code.

  Sub-bead 2: Chat thread storage package (cli/internal/chat/)
    - New Go package: ChatThread, ChatMessage, AppendMessage, LoadThread, ListThreads.
    - Append-only messages.jsonl invariants, atomic manifest writes.
    - Unit tests including concurrent append safety.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 3: GraphQL surface (schema + resolvers + integration tests)
    - schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regen, four resolver files, integration tests.
    - chatSend dispatches via service.Execute and writes the assistant turn as a single message once the session completes (event-level streaming, simplest first cut). Chunk-level streaming is a follow-up.
    - chatStream subscription emits message-created events.
    - Session linkage via SessionIndexEntry.id reference on each assistant message.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 4: Frontend chat pane
    - New ChatPane.svelte component, thread list, message list, input box, harness/profile picker.
    - Mounted on project home page below the actions section.
    - subscribeChatMessages helper in src/lib/gql/subscriptions.ts.
    - Cost rollup in thread header. Stop button calls workerStop on the in-flight session's worker.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 5: Playwright e2e + auth posture middleware
    - Two-context test, cancel test, streaming chunk test (with mocked graphql-ws server).
    - Auth middleware gate stub on chat routes.
    Size: 1 pass.

The bead's AC11 (depends-on ddx-2513f08c, ddx-2ceb02fa) was not verified in this pass. Sub-bead 1 should re-verify those deps before locking the design.

Recommendation: re-queue this bead as a tracking parent and create the five sub-beads above. The current bead description is unusually thorough and most of its content can be lifted verbatim into the design-note sub-bead's body.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-05b4cc9d/events.jsonl:26:{"actor":"ddx","body":"Bead ddx-05b4cc9d: no new changes needed — all AC already satisfied by prior
execute-bead attempts merged to main and present at the base rev of this
worktree (a21580e1).

## What is done

AC1 — Root-cause fix (Option 1, id→path at filter site)
    cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go lines 267-299, landed in commit
    818b5c9e "fix(server): workersByProject filters by project path, not id
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". GetWorkersGraphQL now calls s.GetProjectByID(projectID),
    derives expectedPath = proj.Path, and compares rec.ProjectRoot to the
    resolved path. Unknown project id returns empty (not error).

AC2 — Two-project regression test
    cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go —
    TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (new) seeds two registered projects
    with distinct paths/ids, writes a worker targeting each, and asserts:
      * workersByProject(id=A) returns only worker A
      * workersByProject(id=B) returns only worker B
      * workers (global) returns both
      * workersByProject(id=unknown) returns empty (no GraphQL error)
    TestGraphQLWorkers was also updated to query by resolved project id,
    so it actually exercises the fix.

    Verified locally:
      $ cd cli && go test -run "TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID|TestGraphQLWorkers" ./internal/server/ -v
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkers (0.01s)
      --- PASS: TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID (0.01s)
      ok  github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/server

AC3 — Backward-compat test for pre-migration worker records
    N/A. AC3 is conditional on choosing Option 2 (add ProjectID field to
    WorkerRecord). Option 1 was chosen; WorkerRecord schema is unchanged
    and no migration path exists, so the compatibility concern does not
    apply.

AC4 — Playwright e2e validation
    cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/actions.spec.ts lines 129-226, landed
    in commit 5f234aad "test(ui): cover project-scoped drain worker flow
    [ddx-05b4cc9d]". The test navigates to the project home page, clicks
    Drain queue, confirms the dialog, waits, navigates to the Workers tab,
    and asserts exactly one row with state "running" and the matching
    worker id prefix. Then it navigates to a second project's Workers tab
    and asserts that project-1's worker does not appear and the empty-state
    message is visible.

AC5 — No regression to unrelated surfaces
    * Global workers query still returns all workers — covered by the
      "global workers returns both" assertion in AC2's regression test.
    * Worker detail page loads by worker id — GetWorkerGraphQL(id) was
      not touched by the fix; it keys on worker id, not project id/path.
    * workerProgress subscription patches rows in place — the subscription
      resolver was not modified; only the list-filter comparison changed.

AC6 — Cross-reference to ddx-69789664
    Present in the 818b5c9e commit message:
      "Unblocks the Workers-lifecycle-controls bead (ddx-69789664) whose
       acceptance assumed the Workers pane worked correctly."

## Why nothing changes in this attempt

The bead's <notes> section contains BLOCKING review findings from the first
attempt (before 818b5c9e landed). Attempts 1 and 2 already addressed each
finding:

  818b5c9e — server fix + first regression test (AC1, AC2, AC6)
  5f234aad — Playwright e2e (AC4)

Both commits are ancestors of HEAD (a21580e1):
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 818b5c9e HEAD  # true
  $ git merge-base --is-ancestor 5f234aad HEAD  # true

The files in the worktree already contain the fix and the tests. Running
the tests shows they pass. Re-committing the same changes would produce
an empty diff or duplicate history. A well-justified no-changes is the
correct outcome here; the bead should be closed by the reviewer on the
basis of the evidence already in main.

  # expect the id→path resolution in GetWorkersGraphQL

  cd cli && go test -run TestGraphQLWorkersByProjectScopedByID ./internal/server/ -v
  # expect PASS

Recommended follow-up:

reason: The axon bead backend is not yet implemented, so the benchmark this bead asks for cannot be written end-to-end. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") require an axon Backend implementation; none exists in cli/internal/bead/.

Evidence checked:
- `cli/internal/bead/axon/` does not exist (Glob).
- No `BackendAxon` constant, no `axon.New*`, no `NewAxonBackend` references in cli/internal/bead (Grep).
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` exposes only `BackendJSONL`, `BackendBD`, `BackendBR`.
- TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md) is the design spec; it lists the bench (test plan §4) as a Beta gate.
- Sibling beads under parent ddx-5d49b14e show the prerequisite work is still open:
  * ddx-8d747049 open — "axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client"
  * ddx-95ec5ed5 open — "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"
  * ddx-743bc194 open — "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend"
  * ddx-6602559c open — "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" (duplicate of this bead)

Follow-up: this bead should depend on the axon backend implementation beads (ddx-8d747049 and/or ddx-95ec5ed5) and become ready only once those close. Alternatively, dedupe with ddx-6602559c. A jsonl-only benchmark on the 1100-bead corpus could be filed as a separate bead if a baseline is wanted before axon lands, but the AC as written here cannot be satisfied without the axon backend.

.ddx/attachments/ddx-fdd3ea36/events.jsonl:6:{"actor":"ddx","body":"no_changes
rationale: # No-changes rationale — ddx-fdd3ea36 (routing point release adoption)

This is the second consecutive no_changes for this bead (prior attempt
20260428T222708-3b6ff345 reached the same conclusion). I re-verified the
upstream / DDx state and concur with that prior analysis: the bead is a
multi-PR epic, not a single bounded execute-bead pass, and it should be
split before another implementation attempt.

## What is verified done

1. All six upstream routing-plan beads in `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/` are
   `closed`:
     agent-0dafc7f0, agent-191a74f9, agent-1f46cf22,
     agent-53f38d95, agent-bab52778, agent-dfabb10b.

2. Their closing commits are reachable from the tag DDx already pins:
   `cli/go.mod` → `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23`.
   `git merge-base --is-ancestor <SHA> v0.9.23` succeeds for the
   closing commits of every routing-plan bead checked
   (db26108, b645280, 0bc2bf4, b85e90e). So the upstream binary
   surface AC #1 wants is already on the consumer's classpath; no
   `go.mod` bump is even required.

## Why this still cannot be a single-pass commit

The bead's AC enumerates 10 items spanning D1–D5 plus follow-ups:

  - D1+D2: rip out DDx-side tier-ladder synthesis from the default
    `ddx agent run` / `ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so
    each issues exactly one `ResolveRoute(Profile: "default")` call —
    touches `workerDispatchAdapter`, `execute_bead_loop.go`,
    `agent_cmd.go`, `profile_ladder.go`, `serviceconfig.go`.
  - D3: insert a typed-incompatibility preflight gate before
    `beadStore.Claim` at `execute_bead_loop.go:~352`.
  - D4: deliberate breaking config change — `default_harness` becomes
    a hard error at load; `profile_ladders` and `model_overrides`
    move to opt-in-only with one-time warnings, requires schema
    deletion + migration test + CHANGELOG breaking-change note.
  - D5: CLI override surface — flag plumbing + per-flag e2e tests
    covering harness-only, model-only, harness+model conflict, and
    profile+harness conflict.
  - AC #6: Playwright drain-queue golden matching the user's real
    endpoint config + a "no gemini + non-gemini-model fallback"
    assertion.
  - AC #7: search-and-delete pass for any DDx-side
    allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring re-implementation, plus a
    dead-code detector wired into CI to catch orphans of
    `ResolveProfileLadder` / `ResolveTierModelRef`.
  - AC #8: file follow-up beads for D6 (route-status / providers UI)
    and reaffirm `ddx-05b4cc9d` independence.
  - AC #9: link DDx CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md to upstream docs and write
    DDx CHANGELOG entry.
  - AC #10: net-new regression integration test that reproduces the
    19-burn drain-queue failure mode with the user's exact config —
    requires fixture work (gemini binary presence, minimax override)
    that is itself a sub-bead.

Conservatively: 1–3k LOC across ~10 Go files plus frontend Playwright
fixtures plus CI wiring plus tracker mutations. Crucially D4 is a
deliberate breaking change to user-facing config schema; landing it
without explicit user alignment risks breaking real `.ddx.yml` files
in the wild. The bead description itself describes the work as "one
DDx PR" but the AC list is the union of nine independent
deliverables.

execute-bead's contract is to satisfy AC deterministically, not to
make architecture or breaking-change decisions across nine
sub-deliverables in one pass. A partial commit would fail most AC
items; a sprawling commit would couple breaking schema changes to
mechanical refactors and frontend fixtures, which is exactly the
shape of PR the bead's own sequencing tries to avoid.

## What a follow-up attempt needs

Split this bead into the nine smaller beads listed in the prior
attempt's rationale (`.ddx/executions/20260428T222708-3b6ff345/result.json`,
section "What a follow-up attempt needs"):

  1. routing-bump (trivial; AC #1 — though arguably already
     satisfied by v0.9.23)
  2. routing-default-path (D1 + D2)
  3. routing-preflight-gate (D3)
  4. routing-config-deprecation (D4 — breaking, needs explicit
     user sign-off in its own bead description)
  5. routing-overrides-e2e (D5 + AC #5)
  6. routing-drain-regression (AC #10)
  7. routing-cleanup (AC #7 dead-code detector)
  8. routing-frontend-goldens (AC #6)
  9. routing-followups (AC #8)

Recommendation: reopen this bead with helix-evolve to perform the
split, keep ddx-fdd3ea36 as the epic tracker, and queue beads 2 and 3
first — they unblock G1/G2/G3 with the smallest reasonable PR.

The reduced-scope language in the bead's own description ("DDx-side
tracker only", "blocks on a single upstream point release") confirms
this was meant as an EPIC, not a deliverable. Two consecutive
execute-bead passes have now reached the same conclusion; further
re-execution at higher model tiers will not change the outcome —
the blocker is bead shape, not capability.

rationale: Bead: ddx-fdd3ea36 — Adopt ddx-agent routing point release (DDx-side consumer epic)

Disposition: no_changes — this is an epic/tracker bead whose work has been
deliberately decomposed into nine child beads. Some are closed; four remain
open. The tracker cannot close until those four close. There is no
implementation work that belongs at the epic level itself.

================================================================================
What is done
================================================================================

Upstream prerequisite (AC #1):
  All six routing-plan beads in ~/Projects/agent/.ddx/ are closed and merged
  into tagged ddx-agent releases (v0.9.10..v0.9.24):
    agent-191a74f9  profiles (default/local/standard)         closed
    agent-bab52778  supported-models allow-list + gating      closed
    agent-dfabb10b  ErrHarnessModelIncompatible / PinConflict closed
    agent-53f38d95  public RouteDecision.Candidates           closed
    agent-0dafc7f0  CostUSDPer1kTokens + cost tiebreak        closed
    agent-1f46cf22  routing documentation                     closed
  cli/go.mod pins github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent v0.9.23, which contains
  every routing-plan commit (verified via `git tag --contains <sha>` against
  closing_commit_sha for each upstream bead).

DDx-side child beads already closed:
  ddx-967e9802  routing-bump (go.mod pin + CHANGELOG)         closed
  ddx-755f5881  routing-default-path (D1 + D2)                closed   → AC #2
  ddx-98e6e9ef  routing-preflight-gate (D3)                   closed   → AC #3
  ddx-dd4c423d  routing-drain-regression test                 closed   → AC #10

Code evidence the closed work is in tree:
  cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go:20-21
    test seams verify ResolveProfileLadder/ResolveTierModelRef are NOT called
    on the default path (per ddx-755f5881 AC #3)
  cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:84-120
    DispatchWorker normalizes empty input to {Profile: "default"} only
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:360-392
    routing preflight gate runs BEFORE Claim at line 394 (D3, ddx-98e6e9ef)
  cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1683-1684
    --escalate / --override-model flags exist; tier ladder + model_overrides
    are off by default (consistent with D2/D4 design)
  cli/internal/config/types.go:230-234
    migration note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in ddx-87fb72c2

================================================================================
What is open (blocks tracker close)
================================================================================

ddx-87fb72c2  routing-config-deprecation                      open  → AC #4
              hard error on default_harness; ladders/overrides opt-in only;
              CHANGELOG migration entry. (Note: the field-removal half appears
              landed per types.go:230-234 — verify what residual scope this
              bead still owns before executing it.)

ddx-2589e256  routing-overrides-e2e                           open  → AC #5
              --harness/--model/--profile flag plumbing parity tests for
              ResolveRoute(Profile, Harness, Model) shape; cost-tiebreak e2e;
              typed-error surfacing for incompatible combos.

ddx-950321ef  routing-frontend-goldens                        open  → AC #6
              Playwright drain-queue fixtures matching the real failing
              config; Drain button → live-endpoint-backed harness;
              never gemini+non-gemini-model; never 19-burn.

ddx-653f6ac9  routing-cleanup                                 open  → AC #7
              dead-code detector for orphaned compensating logic;
              remove residual allow-list / exact-pin / cost-scoring code
              that re-implements upstream behavior.

ddx-a0cf398b  routing-followups                               open  → AC #8
              file the route-status / providers-UI decision-trace consumer
              bead; close out D6/D7 housekeeping.

================================================================================
Why this bead does not get implementation in this pass
================================================================================

1. The epic's AC is the union of its children's AC. Every remaining AC item
   (#4, #5, #6, #7, #8, plus the documentation half of #9) is the contract of
   a specific open child bead. Implementing them here would either duplicate
   work that the child bead will redo, or steal scope that another worker
   may already be planning to claim.

2. The bead description's "Sequencing" section explicitly orders:
     1. upstream lands (done)
     2. go.mod bump (done — ddx-967e9802)
     3. D1+D2+D3+D5 land together (D1+D2=ddx-755f5881 done; D3=ddx-98e6e9ef
        done; D5=ddx-2589e256 OPEN)
     4. D4 (ddx-87fb72c2) lands with or right after the above (OPEN)
     5. follow-ups (ddx-a0cf398b, ddx-950321ef, ddx-653f6ac9) (OPEN)
   The tracker is meant to close once steps 3–5 complete via their child
   beads, not to carry independent code.

3. Attempting any of {D4, override e2e, playwright goldens, dead-code sweep}
   in this single execute-bead pass would land a sprawling cross-cutting
   commit that violates the per-child-bead acceptance contracts and is
   hard to review. Per CLAUDE.md "DOWITYTD" and the merge-policy memory,
   the right action is to execute the children individually.

================================================================================
What a follow-up attempt should do
================================================================================

This tracker should NOT be re-queued for direct execution. Instead:

  a) Execute the four open child beads in their natural order:
        ddx-87fb72c2 (D4 — config deprecation residue + CHANGELOG)
        ddx-2589e256 (D5 — override flag e2e)
        ddx-950321ef (D6-equivalent — Playwright goldens)
        ddx-653f6ac9 (cleanup / dead-code sweep)
        ddx-a0cf398b (file follow-up beads)
     Each closes one or two AC items on this tracker.

  b) When all five close, re-open this tracker briefly to:
        - verify every AC item maps to a closed child or in-tree evidence
        - update DDx CHANGELOG (AC #9) if not already done by ddx-967e9802
        - close ddx-fdd3ea36 with a closing-comment that points at each
          child bead's closing commit.

That close step is documentation/bookkeeping — a single small commit, well
suited to a future execute-bead pass once the children are green.

No code changes were made in this pass. Workspace is clean.
