name to prevent SQL injection in DDL.\\n///\\n/// PostgreSQL identifiers may contain letters, digits, `_`, and `$`, and\\n/// must start with a letter or `_`. We apply a conservative subset of that\\n/// rule here to keep things simple and safe.\\nfn validate_pg_db_name(name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    if name.is_empty() {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(\\n            \\\"database name must not be empty\\\".to_owned(),\\n        ));\\n    }\\n    let first = name.chars().next().expect(\\\"non-empty checked above\\\");\\n    if !first.is_ascii_alphabetic() \u0026\u0026 first != '_' {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' must start with a letter or underscore\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    if !name.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_') {\\n        return Err(AxonError::InvalidArgument(format!(\\n            \\\"database name '{name}' contains invalid characters (only ASCII alphanumeric and _ allowed)\\\"\\n        )));\\n    }\\n    Ok(())\\n}\\n\\n/// Derive a per-tenant DSN from a superadmin DSN by replacing/adding the\\n/// `dbname` key.\\n///\\n/// The superadmin DSN uses the `postgres` maintenance database.  We produce a\\n/// new DSN that targets `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// Both libpq keyword–value format (`host=... dbname=...`) and URL format\\n/// (`postgres://...`) are supported.\\npub fn tenant_dsn(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, tenant_db_name: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    let target = format!(\\\"axon_{tenant_db_name}\\\");\\n    if superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgres://\\\") || superadmin_dsn.starts_with(\\\"postgresql://\\\") {\\n        // URL format: replace path component.\\n        // e.g. postgres://user:pass@host/somedb?sslmode=disable\\n        if let Some(pos) = superadmin_dsn.find(\\\"://\\\") {\\n            let after_scheme = \u0026superadmin_dsn[pos + 3..];\\n            // Find the slash that separates authority from path.\\n            if let Some(slash_pos) = after_scheme.find('/') {\\n                let scheme_authority = \u0026superadmin_dsn[..pos + 3 + slash_pos];\\n                let rest = \u0026after_scheme[slash_pos + 1..];\\n                // Strip existing dbname (everything before '?').\\n                let query = if let Some(q) = rest.find('?') {\\n                    \u0026rest[q..]\\n                } else {\\n                    \\\"\\\"\\n                };\\n                return format!(\\\"{scheme_authority}/{target}{query}\\\");\\n            }\\n            // No path: append the target database.\\n            return format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\");\\n        }\\n        format!(\\\"{superadmin_dsn}/{target}\\\")\\n    } else {\\n        // Keyword–value format.  Replace existing dbname= or append it.\\n        let mut parts: Vec\u003cString\u003e = superadmin_dsn\\n            .split_whitespace()\\n            .filter(|part| !part.starts_with(\\\"dbname=\\\"))\\n            .map(str::to_owned)\\n            .collect();\\n        parts.push(format!(\\\"dbname={target}\\\"));\\n        parts.join(\\\" \\\")\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n/// Create a physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}` using a\\n/// superadmin connection.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must connect to the `postgres` maintenance database\\n/// (or any database the superuser can reach) with sufficient privileges to\\n/// execute `CREATE DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// The database name is validated to prevent SQL injection.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::AlreadyExists` if the database already exists.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn provision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::AlreadyExists(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        // CREATE DATABASE cannot run inside a transaction; use simple_query to\\n        // avoid the implicit transaction that execute() would open.\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"CREATE DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n/// Drop the physical PostgreSQL database named `axon_{name}`.\\n///\\n/// The `superadmin_dsn` must have sufficient privileges to execute\\n/// `DROP DATABASE`.\\n///\\n/// # Errors\\n///\\n/// Returns `AxonError::NotFound` if the database does not exist.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::InvalidArgument` if `name` contains invalid characters.\\n/// Returns `AxonError::Storage` for other PostgreSQL errors.\\npub fn deprovision_postgres_database(superadmin_dsn: \u0026str, name: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), AxonError\u003e {\\n    validate_pg_db_name(name)?;\\n    let full_name = format!(\\\"axon_{name}\\\");\\n    let rt = tokio::runtime::Builder::new_current_thread()\\n        .enable_all()\\n        .build()\\n        .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n    rt.block_on(async {\\n        let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(superadmin_dsn, NoTls)\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\\n            let _ = connection.await;\\n        });\\n        let row = client\\n            .query_one(\\n                \\\"SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM pg_database WHERE datname = $1)\\\",\\n                \u0026[\u0026full_name.as_str()],\\n            )\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        let exists: bool = row.get(0);\\n        if !exists {\\n            return Err(AxonError::NotFound(format!(\\n                \\\"PostgreSQL database '{full_name}'\\\"\\n            )));\\n        }\\n        client\\n            .simple_query(\u0026format!(\\\"DROP DATABASE \\\\\\\"{full_name}\\\\\\\"\\\"))\\n            .await\\n            .map_err(|e| AxonError::Storage(e.to_string()))?;\\n        Ok(())\\n    })\\n}\\n\\n// The conformance test suite requires PostgreSQL.\\n// Explicit verification path:\\n// Clock-skew boundary at ±30 s\\n//! - HTTP-method × grant-ops matrix\\n\\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\\nuse std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\\n\\nuse axum::body::Body;\\nuse axum::extract::Extension;\\nuse axum::http::StatusCode;\\nuse axum::response::IntoResponse;\\nuse axum::routing::get;\\nuse axum::Router;\\nuse http::Request;\\nuse serde::Serialize;\\nuse serde_json::Value;\\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\\nuse uuid::Uuid;\\n\\nuse axon_core::auth::{\\n    AuthError, GrantedDatabase, Grants, JwtClaims, Op, ResolvedIdentity, TenantId, TenantMember,\\n    TenantRole, User, UserId,\\n};\\nuse axon_server::auth_pipeline::{\\n    AuthErrorResponse, AuthPipelineState, InMemoryRevocationCache, JwtIssuer,\\n    jwt_verify_layer,\\n};\\nuse axon_storage::MemoryStorageAdapter;\\n\\n// ── Test helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\nconst ISSUER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"test-issuer\\\";\\nconst TENANT: \u0026str = \\\"acme\\\";\\nconst DATABASE: \u0026str = \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"user-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"super-secret-key-for-tests\\\";\\nconst TEST_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_claims() -\u003e JwtClaims {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER_ID.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants: Grants {\\n            databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n                name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n                ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write],\\n            }],\\n        },\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nfn make_storage() -\u003e MemoryStorageAdapter {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Test User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    storage\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let storage: Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e =\\n        Box::new(make_storage());\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(issuer),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(storage)),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(TEST_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service call should succeed\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer_req(path: \u0026str, token: \u0026str) -\u003e Request\u003cBody\u003e {\\n    Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(path)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n    json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\")\\n}\\n\\n// ── JWT round-trip ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds()      .await\\n        .expect(\\\"body read should succeed\\\");\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn get_req(path: format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\"))\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap()\\n}\\n\\nfn error_code(json: \u0026Value) -\u003e \u0026str {\\n storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Unknown paths fall back to `index.html`\\n28\\t/// so SvelteKit's client-side router can handle them.\\n29\\tpub async fn embedded_ui_handler(uri: Uri) -\u003e Response {\\n30\\t    let raw = uri.path();\\n31\\t    // Strip the /ui prefix that axum leaves on the URI when not using nest_service.\\n32\\t    let asset_path = raw\\n33\\t        .strip_prefix(\\\"/ui/\\\")\\n34\\t        .or_else(|| raw.strip_prefix(\\\"/ui\\\"))\\n35\\t        .unwrap_or(raw);\\n36\\t    let asset_path = if asset_path.is_empty() {\\n37\\t        \\\"index.html\\\"\\n38\\t    } else {\\n39\\t        asset_path\\n40\\t    };\\n41\\t\\n42\\t    serve_asset(asset_path)\\n43\\t}\\n44\\t\\n45\\tfn serve_asset(path: \u0026str) -\u003e Response {\\n46\\t    match UiAssets::get(path) {\\n47\\t        Some(content) =\u003e {\\n48\\t            let mime = mime_guess::from_path(path).first_or_octet_stream();\\n49\\t            Response::builder()\\n50\\t                .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, mime.as_ref())\\n51\\t                .body(Body::from(content.data))\\n52\\t                .unwrap_or_else(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response())\\n53\\t        }\\n54\\t        None =\u003e {\\n55\\t            // SPA fallback: let the client-side router handle unknown paths.\\n56\\t            match UiAssets::get(\\\"index.html\\\") {\\n57\\t                Some(index) =\u003e Response::builder()\\n58\\t                    .header(header::CONTENT_TYPE, \\\"text/html; {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = valid_claims();\\n    let token = issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\");\\n    let decoded = issuer.verify(\u0026token).expect(\\\"verify should succeed\\\");\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.iss, claims.iss);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.sub, claims.sub);\\n    assert_eq!(decoded.aud, directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. data.text: There’s already a store-level ancestor guard for `DepAdd`; I’m checking whether `Create` reuses it or silently rewires dependencies during parent assignment. If `Create` bypasses the same invariant, I’ll centralize the check there and let both CLI surfaces inherit it.

data.output:
package cmd

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)

	parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)

	t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})

	t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})
}

data.text: The store-level invariant is already present, so I’m checking for any alternate write path that can bypass it. If there is one, the right fix is to route all dependency writes through the same ancestor check instead of duplicating logic in each caller.

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(workingDir), 0o755))
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "backend,urgent", "--acceptance", "bug is fixed")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	createdID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, createdID)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"))

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var created map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &created))
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, created["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, "Fix auth bug", created["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", created["issue_type"])
	assert.Equal(t, "open", created["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), created["priority"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", createdID, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me", "--labels", "backend")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", updated["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", updated["owner"])
	require.Len(t, updated["labels"], 1)

	listOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "list", "--status", "in_progress", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var listed []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &listed))
	require.Len(t, listed, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, listed[0]["id"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", createdID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	statusOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var status map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(statusOut), &status))
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["closed"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), status["open"])
}

func TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Transition target", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "blocked")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "transition_requires_external_blocker")

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var got map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &got))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", got["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", got["owner"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimUsesExplicitAssignee(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim", "--assignee", "alice")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", bead["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "alice", bead["owner"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, bead["claimed-at"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, bead["claimed-pid"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimFallsBackToCallerIdentity(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	t.Setenv("USER", "runtime-agent")

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me too", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "runtime-agent", bead["owner"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsUnsetCustomField(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Replay provenance", "--type", "task", "--set", "closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88", bead["closing_commit_sha"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--unset", "closing_commit_sha")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	_, ok := updated["closing_commit_sha"]
	assert.False(t, ok)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCanNormalizeClosingCommitShaOnClosedBead(t *testing.T) {
	env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
	env.CreateConfig(`version: "1.0"
library:
  path: "./library"
  repository:
    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
    branch: "main"
git:
  auto_commit: always
  commit_prefix: beads
`)
	gitAddAndCommit(t, env.Dir, "track ddx config", ".ddx/config.yaml")

	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, env.Dir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Replay provenance", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", id)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	fullSHA := gitHead(t, env.Dir, "HEAD")
	shortSHA := gitShortHead(t, env.Dir)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--set", "closing_commit_sha="+shortSHA)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "closed", bead["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, fullSHA, bead["closing_commit_sha"])
}

data.text: I’ve confirmed the store layer already rejects ancestor back-edges. The remaining risk is the decomposition workflow itself: if it synthesizes child dependencies from the parent chain incorrectly, the store guard will only fail late. I’m tracing that path now.

data.text: I’m at the point where the only defensible edit is test coverage. The implementation already rejects the bad edges at the store layer, so I’m checking the command-test helper shape and then I’ll add a CLI-level `dep add` regression test that exercises the same ancestor-chain error path.

data.output:
package cmd

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)

	parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)

	t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})

	t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})
}

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-63780ef6">     <title>Reject bead dep edges that re-introduce the parent or any ancestor: part 1</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-e19185aa is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
The decomposition action (currently invoked when a bead is too large for one execution and the agent splits it into child beads) is writing the parent's ID as a dep edge on every child. This produces an unbreakable deadlock:

  e29892a4 (parent, open) -- parent --> child_A
  e29892a4 -- parent --> child_B
  child_A.deps = [e29892a4]           <- BAD: parent is also a dep
  child_B.deps = [child_A, e29892a4]  <- BAD

The parent stays open until its children close. Every child is blocked on the parent. Nothing can be claimed. ddx work loops on the parent, the orchestrator emits 'mixed_commit_and_no_changes_rationale', the worker retries, and burns claim budget without progress.

Observed scenario (niflheim, 2026-06-04 04:30-05:13 UTC):
- Parent: niflheim-e29892a4 ('feat(delta): async write-behind ...')
- 10 children created during planning loops (23223661, a44a8ceb, 054c89b7, 0470ba0d, f10e9e35, f076b08c, 6c041162, 94e1df73, 9da62c2d, 5e4f9fb2)
- Every child carried deps=[..siblings.., niflheim-e29892a4]
- ~50 minutes of agent time + 4 strong-policy claude/opus attempts with zero feature commits; only tracker chores
- Unwedged manually by closing the parent via 'ddx bead close', after which siblings drained correctly in foundational order

Root cause is structural, not model-capability — auto-upgrading to a stronger model does NOT fix this. The orchestrator already detects the no-progress state but its only remediation is 'retry parent', which re-enters the same loop.

There is also a 'project root mutation rejected (continuing)' warning emitted by the readiness check during this state; that's a downstream symptom of the same problem, not the cause.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
- 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. 'ddx bead dep add <bead> <dep>' HARD-REJECTS the edge when <dep> is <bead>'s parent OR any ancestor in the parent chain. Error message names the offending ancestor and prints the relevant parent-chain. Test: ddx_bead_dep_add_rejects_parent_ancestor.
2. 'ddx bead create --depends-on X --parent Y' with X == Y (or X an ancestor of Y) HARD-REJECTS at creation time with the same error shape as AC #1. No silent rewrite.
3. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

I couldn’t complete this bead in a way I’d trust.

What I verified:
- The ancestor-dependency invariant already exists in `Store.Create` and `Store.DepAdd` in [`cli/internal/bead/store.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63780ef6-20260607T033207-f897f09d/cli/internal/bead/store.go).
- The CLI create path already has a regression test for the ancestor-chain rejection in [`cli/cmd/bead_create_dep_test.go`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63780ef6-20260607T033207-f897f09d/cli/cmd/bead_create_dep_test.go).

What blocked completion:
- `cd cli && go test ./...` fails in unrelated performance suites:
  - `cli/internal/bead/store_perf_test.go`
  - `cli/internal/server/graphql_bead_lookup_perf_test.go`
- Because AC #3 is red in this environment, I did not commit anything.

Evidence written:
- [`no_changes_rationale.txt`](/Users/erik/Projects/.ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-63780ef6-20260607T033207-f897f09d/.ddx/executions/20260607T033207-f897f09d/no_changes_rationale.txt)

If you want, I can take one of these next steps:
1. Retry on a less loaded machine so the full `go test ./...` budget tests have a chance to pass.
2. Narrow the verification to the affected bead packages and then add a separate note that the perf suites are pre-existing blockers.
3. Investigate the two failing perf tests and see whether they need threshold tuning or an environment guard.
